scope: Recent MIT HAN Lab frontier systems and attention-efficiency work that complements the older efficient-ML core pack. These entries focus on sparse attention, streaming, and long-context inference patterns relevant to control-plane serving and future student efficiency research.
source_policy: Primary sources only: official MIT HAN Lab papers, repositories, and project pages.
design_laws: Most of this pack applies first to the control plane, large teachers, or future student efficiency work, not the immediate perception baseline.
Kernel-level wins only matter after the architectural reason for using the attention pattern is clear.
Do not transplant long-context inference tricks into the perception stack without a matching bottleneck.

entries.title: Quest
entries.core_trick: Exploit query-aware sparsity to prune attention work adaptively during long-context inference.
entries.intuition: Quest is useful when queries clearly reveal which context regions matter and the system is bottlenecked by long-context inference cost, not by missing knowledge.
ablation_takeaways: The win comes from pruning by relevance to the current query rather than using a static sparsity mask.
ablation_nuggets: Use for long-context planner/critic serving if retrieval length becomes the new bottleneck.
apply_when: control-plane or large-teacher inference is query-dependent and long-context bound
avoid_when: the active problem is multimodal alignment in perception, not long-context reasoning throughput
entries.integration_pattern: Apply to planner/critic or giant teacher serving before attempting any perception-side attention rewrite.
failure_modes: sparsifying context before the retrieval stack is even relevant enough
treating a serving optimization as a model-quality improvement
tsqbev_actions: reserve for planner/critic scaling, not for the current local perception frontier

entries.title: XAttention
entries.core_trick: Score block importance with antidiagonal statistics so nonessential attention blocks can be pruned aggressively during long-context inference.
entries.intuition: XAttention is a fine-grained sparse attention mechanism for cases where block-level structure in the attention map is predictable enough to prune safely.
ablation_takeaways: The antidiagonal block proxy is the key insight; the gain is not from generic sparsity alone.
ablation_nuggets: Useful when a long-context serving stack needs more aggressive block pruning than simple cache tricks.
apply_when: planner/critic or giant-teacher attention maps are structured enough for block-level pruning
avoid_when: the current bottleneck is still the perception student rather than the control plane
entries.integration_pattern: Use as a later serving optimization layer after basic retrieval and reranking are already strong.
failure_modes: spending kernel engineering budget before the architectural bottleneck is proven
pruning too aggressively when attention structure is not stable
tsqbev_actions: use only after the knowledge stack and control plane justify sparse long-context serving work

entries.title: Block-Sparse-Attention
entries.core_trick: Provide a sparse attention kernel that supports mixed sparsity patterns instead of forcing one rigid masking rule.
entries.intuition: A good sparse-attention idea is not enough; the kernel decides whether it becomes an actual system win. Mixed-pattern support matters when multiple sparse strategies coexist.
ablation_takeaways: Kernel support is the difference between conceptual sparsity and practical throughput.
ablation_nuggets: Use only after the attention pattern itself is justified by the model bottleneck.
apply_when: a sparse attention design is already chosen and mixed mask patterns matter at runtime
avoid_when: you have not yet justified sparse attention architecturally
entries.integration_pattern: Apply as a later kernel backend for long-context reasoning or giant-teacher inference, not as the first perception rewrite.
failure_modes: assuming the same sparse pattern fits both reasoning and perception
tsqbev_actions: consider only if the planner/critic or giant teachers move onto a sparse-attention serving stack

entries.title: FlashMoBA
entries.core_trick: Make mixture-of-block attention memory efficient and practical by building it directly on top of optimized FlashAttention-style kernels.
entries.intuition: MoBA-style attention only matters if the implementation preserves the intended memory savings and throughput. FlashMoBA is the systems realization, not just the paper concept.
ablation_takeaways: Kernel-level realization is essential for mixture-of-block attention to matter in practice.
ablation_nuggets: Use after a mixture-of-block attention design is justified, not before.
apply_when: a large reasoning or teacher stack already benefits from MoBA-style sparse patterns
avoid_when: there is no proven need for MoBA-like sparse attention in the current stack
entries.integration_pattern: Late-stage serving/kernel optimization for giant teachers or control-plane reasoning models.
failure_modes: trying to solve a student-side supervision problem with an inference kernel
tsqbev_actions: keep in reserve for giant-teacher serving, not the current perception stack

entries.title: StreamingVLM
entries.core_trick: Support real-time understanding over effectively unbounded video streams instead of treating every clip as a fixed offline batch.
entries.intuition: Streaming multimodal models matter when the control plane or teacher must reason continuously over video instead of resetting on short windows.
ablation_takeaways: Streaming wins show up when context truly never stops; they are not free gains for short static clips.
ablation_nuggets: Useful for future control-plane or teacher-side continuous video reasoning, not for the local student detector.
apply_when: the research lab needs persistent video understanding over long streams
avoid_when: the active frontier is still per-frame or short-sequence 3D detection
entries.integration_pattern: Use in the control plane or giant teacher stack when long video context becomes a real research bottleneck.
failure_modes: overengineering streaming before the underlying perception model is strong
assuming streaming context automatically improves 3D metrics
tsqbev_actions: defer until long-horizon video reasoning is the actual lab bottleneck

entries.title: SpAtten
entries.core_trick: Jointly prune tokens and heads with hardware-aware sparse attention design so the attention path becomes feasible on efficient hardware.
entries.intuition: SpAtten is a reminder that sparse attention becomes truly useful only when the architecture and hardware story agree.
ablation_takeaways: Pruning and hardware co-design matter together; one without the other underdelivers.
ablation_nuggets: Use as design inspiration for deployment-aware attention, not as a drop-in perception fix.
apply_when: hardware-aware sparse attention is a real deployment requirement
avoid_when: the stack has not yet proven which attention path deserves hardware specialization
entries.integration_pattern: Deployment-stage inspiration for sparse attention hardware co-design, after the architectural frontier is settled.
failure_modes: overfitting attention design to a hardware assumption too early
tsqbev_actions: keep as a later deployment specialization reference, not as an immediate student-side experiment
